ThingDef.label: underground emergence
ThingDef.description: Something emerging from ground.

ThingDef.label: Root Fleshmass
ThingDef.description: A thick root mass of living flesh that anchors colony tissue and supports nearby organic growth.

ThingDef.label: reinforced fleshmass wall
ThingDef.description: A thickened mass of colony flesh threaded with rigid bone structure, grown from an ordinary fleshmass wall to hold the colony's perimeter longer under attack.

ThingDef.label: Colony Heart
ThingDef.description: The pulsating core of a fleshmass colony, this organic structure rhythmically pumps hemogel to sustain and interconnect its living architecture. Its vitality hinges on a robust hemogel reserve, driving its ceaseless, life-giving pulsations.

ThingDef.label: Colony Brain
ThingDef.description: The neural command hub of a fleshmass colony. This twitching, bioelectric organ processes stimuli from across the colony’s living structures, issuing signals through fleshy tendrils and hemogel pulses. It grows in complexity with the colony itself, embodying its will and instinct.

ThingDef.label: Colony Stomach
ThingDef.description: A sprawling pit with walls of thick, mucous flesh, teeming with life. Within its depths churns a caustic, greenish gastric juice, breaking down organic matter into hemogel to nourish the fleshmass colony. Sensitive tendrils line the edges, drawing in discarded bodies or debris with relentless precision, fueling the colony’s vitality.

ThingDef.label: gastric acid spewer
ThingDef.description: A rooted digestive organ that spreads gastric acid nearby. Organic bodies and food left in the connected acid pool slowly feed the hemogel network.

ThingDef.label: Hemogel Sac
ThingDef.description: A reserve organ that stores excess heart hemogel and returns it when the colony network runs low.

ThingDef.label: Defender Incubator
ThingDef.description: A defensive incubator that spends hemogel to release short-lived fleshbeast defenders on command or when a hostile enters its detection radius.

ThingDef.label: Fleshleaf
ThingDef.description: A photosynthetic flesh organ that converts unroofed daylight into hemogel for the colony network. Partial roofing reduces output.

ThingDef.label: Fleshtree
ThingDef.description: A compact organ-tree fused from five fleshleaves. It produces about four leaves' worth of hemogel from daylight while occupying far less space and requiring less upkeep.

ThingDef.label: Tissue incubator
ThingDef.description: A living tissue incubator that memorizes delivered meat and leather samples, then grows matching materials from hemogel inside sealed, pulsing chambers.

ThingDef.label: capillary trawler
ThingDef.description: A filter-organ that casts fine vascular nets through nearby water, converting aquatic biomass into steady hemogel output.

ThingDef.label: galvanic cyst
ThingDef.description: A fist-sized knot of electrocyte tissue that metabolizes hemogel into bioelectric charge. It stores the charge in swollen organic cells and stops feeding when its inner reserve is full.

ThingDef.label: geoferrite gland
ThingDef.description: A geyser-rooted organ that consumes hemogel and extrudes bioferrite from geothermal pressure.
An organic feeding structure that converts hemogel into edible biomass. Hungry colonists can approach from any open side and draw a fresh portion directly from the feeder.
ThingDef.label: Feeder
flesh door
ThingDef.description: A segment of living wall formed from dense flesh, this door-like structure is embedded with responsive nerve clusters. When touched, it parts to allow passage, then closes seamlessly behind, restoring structural cohesion. Though it offers little in terms of security, its integration with surrounding tissue makes it ideal for preserving the colony’s living environment.

li.commandDesc: Transforms this flesh door into a throbbing mass of twisted flesh.

ThingDef.label: Flesh Pillar
ThingDef.description: A sinewy stalk of dense flesh that extends from floor to ceiling, throbbing subtly with internal tension. Its fibrous mass distributes weight evenly, allowing nearby structures to remain stable under pressure.

ThingDef.label: fleshbulb
ThingDef.description: A bulbous mass of flesh. These fleshbeast organs store and refine nutrients for surrounding fleshmass. The chemical process generates a soft bioluminescent glow.

A semi-living organ of the colony that cocoons a pawn beneath a thick neuro-membrane. The pawn's brain becomes a conduit for research, feeding thought-signals into the colony's fleshmind. While inside, the pawn cannot act, but their cognitive power accelerates technological advancement.
ThingDef.label: Synapse Core
Flesh Shelter
ThingDef.description: A living bed that shelters a resting pawn, feeds them through a nutrient drip, and tends bleeding wounds when assigned for medical use.

ThingDef.label: Storage Cavity
ThingDef.description: A fleshy, ribbed structure with organic recesses. This shelf-like node stores items securely within moist, flexible tissue. Contents remain protected from decay and weathering, and blend visually with the living colony. Not suitable for heavy or bulky items like chunks, buildings, plants, or large corpses.

ThingDef.label: Spore-Cradle
ThingDef.description: A bio-organic workstation where colonists shape raw biomass into embryonic spore-seeds. At its center pulsates a semi-transparent dome filled with nutrient gel, regulated by fleshy tendrils. When a colonist interacts with it, organic processes are triggered to imprint the blueprint of a chosen item into the seed, which can later be planted to grow equipment within the colony.

ThingDef.label: floor maw trap
ThingDef.description: A living mouth hidden in the floor. It snaps shut on intruders with a wet, bone-cracking bite.

ThingDef.label: mucous decontaminator
ThingDef.description: A living decontaminator. A wide carpet of semi-transparent veins spreads across nearby tiles and converges into a central muscular sphincter. It slowly cleans polluted terrain and periodically compresses to expel soft waste cysts that must be hauled away.

ThingDef.label: hemogel blister
ThingDef.description: A swollen hemogel blister filled with pressurized hemogel. If flames get too close or the organ is destroyed, it bursts in a heavy spray that drenches the surrounding area before fire can spread through living tissue.

ThingDef.label: Thermoregulator
ThingDef.description: A compact living heat-exchange organ. It steadily regulates the surrounding air by pulsing warm hemogel through layered tissue, smoothing temperature swings in enclosed rooms. The core throbs faintly as it adapts, releasing or absorbing heat to maintain a stable, habitable climate.

ThingDef.label: live cocoon growth
ThingDef.description: A living wall-bloom of fleshmass—an anchored cocoon that swells within the colony’s walls. When needed, it can rupture and release a small flesh defender to aid the colony. Its membrane stays tense and quiet while it accumulates hemogel reserves.

ThingDef.label: sun flower
ThingDef.description: A towering chitin-fleshed flower that unfurls into a full crown. At its center burns a pulsing, sun-bright core — a living organ that floods the area with daylike radiance and lets crops grow where true sunlight cannot reach. When the colony’s will quiets, the petals fold over the core, sealing the glow behind a protective husk.

ThingDef.label: Stone Crusher
ThingDef.description: A living grinder that chews stone chunks into usable blocks.

ThingDef.label: Grasping Tendrils
ThingDef.description: A sudden bloom of living tendrils forced up through the ground. They coil and cinch without warning, hauling the victim into a choking knot of flesh and root-fibers. The colony’s hunger works quickly — once the grip is spent, the growth withers and sinks back into the soil.

ThingDef.label: nerve core
ThingDef.description: A quivering neural organ embedded within the fleshmass, this structure coordinates the infection and transformation of living beings. When approached, it lashes out with tendrils, pulling victims into brief neural fusion. Those who survive the contact are forever changed — the first step in serving the will of the colony.

ThingDef.label: colony eye
ThingDef.description: A sensory organ grown from colony flesh. Its wet eye tracks movement around the colony and marks the area as watched by the living mass.

ThingDef.label: biomass cocoon
ThingDef.description: A pulsating cocoon of biomass that holds a colonist, evolving them within.

ThingDef.label: gene reweaver cocoon
ThingDef.description: A temporary genetic cocoon grown by a gene reweaver. It holds a pawn while the prepared flesh xenogerm integrates into the body.

ThingDef.label: minions hive
ThingDef.description: A hive of minions is an organic structure created by carriers of the HiveConstructor gene. This hive spawns small carnivorous creatures known as sclerats, which perform a variety of labor-intensive tasks such as building, dismantling, and transporting materials, greatly improving the colony's efficiency and productivity.

ThingDef.label: symbiosis brood womb
ThingDef.description: A swollen, pulsating womb-node grown from hemogel-fed biomass. It stores harvested skill essence and distills it into living defenders, while a bound maw scavenges nearby corpses to keep the node nourished.

ThingDef.label: genophage cocoon
ThingDef.description: A genetic cocoon that swallows a living donor with genes, stores a few extracted genes as a genepack in a genome archive, then releases the donor once extraction is complete.

ThingDef.label: genome archive
ThingDef.description: A living gene bank that stores extracted genepacks inside organic memory tissue. Its contents can be inspected directly and used by a gene reweaver.

ThingDef.label: gene reweaver
ThingDef.description: A large organic gene loom that recombines archived genepacks into an internal prepared flesh xenogerm, then grows a temporary cocoon to integrate the selected xenogenes into a chosen pawn.

ThingDef.label: deep maw trap
ThingDef.description: A buried colony mouth that wakes when hostile movement presses its hidden nerves. After a short delay, the maw opens and swallows every living body above it, digesting flesh into hemogel while grinding mechanoids apart before spitting their remains back out.

ThingDef.label: capture nerve node
ThingDef.description: A small relay-organ of wet nerves and gripping reflex tissue. It links to the nearest colony heart, expands the heart's grasping tendril capacity, and can manually raise tendrils through that heart's capture network.

ThingDef.label: flesh holding platform
ThingDef.description: A living restraint platform that secures captured entities with vascular tendrils and feeds its grip from the colony's hemogel network.

ThingDef.label: bone marrow extractor
ThingDef.description: A rooted extraction organ that strains captive entities into bioferrite and a small flow of hemogel. Its feeding spines make containment less stable.

ThingDef.label: synaptic inhibitor
ThingDef.description: A small nerve-anchor that dampens the activity of nearby captive entities, improving containment while it receives hemogel.

ThingDef.label: psychic lure antenna
ThingDef.description: An organic antenna. When active, it emits waves of psychoactive hum, drawing the attention of raiders and provoking aggression in animals. Make sure the colony is ready to defend itself.

ThingDef.label: celestial umbilicus
ThingDef.description: A colossal living column grown to feed the colony's final budding. Once charged with hemogel and awakened, it begins pulling a cyclopean vein toward orbit, turning every faction hostile until the flesh colony tears itself free as a new cosmic body.

ThingDef.label: anchored cortex mite
ThingDef.description: A cortex mite fixed into the flesh network, feeding the colony's current research project with a steady neural trickle.
